features: GET /api/conversations — list all conversations sorted by updated_at DESC
POST /api/conversations — create a new empty conversation with UUID and timestamps
GET /api/conversations/{id} — retrieve conversation metadata plus full message history
DELETE /api/conversations/{id} — delete conversation and all associated messages via cascade
POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages — full RAG orchestration: save user message, embed query, retrieve top-5 chunks, build prompt, stream SSE response, persist assistant message
Auto-generate conversation title from first user message (truncate or quick LLM call, ≤50 chars)
Update conversations.updated_at on every new message
GET /api/videos — list all ingested videos with id, title, description, url, created_at
404 handling for unknown conversation IDs across all /conversations/{id} routes

criteria.name: list_conversations_sorted
criteria.description: GET /api/conversations returns a JSON array of all conversations ordered by updated_at descending; after creating two conversations and sending a message to the older one, it appears first in the list

criteria.name: list_conversations_empty_state
criteria.description: GET /api/conversations returns an empty JSON array [] (not null, not a missing field, not an object wrapper) with HTTP status 200 when no conversations exist in the database

criteria.name: create_conversation_shape
criteria.description: POST /api/conversations returns a JSON object with fields: id (UUID v4 string), title (string), created_at (ISO 8601 timestamp), updated_at (ISO 8601 timestamp); HTTP status 201; additionally, created_at and updated_at must be equal at the moment of creation

criteria.name: get_conversation_with_messages
criteria.description: GET /api/conversations/{id} returns a JSON object containing conversation metadata AND a messages array; each message includes id, conversation_id, role ('user' or 'assistant'), content, and created_at; messages are ordered by created_at ascending; for a brand-new conversation with no messages the messages field must be an empty array [], not null or absent

criteria.name: delete_conversation_cascade
criteria.description: DELETE /api/conversations/{id} returns HTTP 204; a subsequent GET /api/conversations/{id} returns HTTP 404; a subsequent GET /api/conversations does not include the deleted conversation's id in any returned object; cascade deletion of associated messages is verified by confirming the conversation no longer appears and re-creating a conversation with the same id is not possible (UUIDs are unique)

criteria.name: conversation_not_found_404
criteria.description: GET, DELETE, and POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages all return HTTP 404 with a JSON error body when the conversation ID does not exist in the database; this must also hold for a well-formed UUID v4 that was never inserted, not just malformed IDs

criteria.name: message_request_body_validation
criteria.description: POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages returns HTTP 422 with a JSON error body containing a 'detail' key when: (a) the request body is missing entirely, (b) the content field is absent, or (c) the content field is an empty string; the response must never be a 500 for these inputs

criteria.name: message_send_persists_user_message
criteria.description: After POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages with a user message body, the message with role='user' is retrievable via GET /api/conversations/{id} with the correct content before or after the stream completes

criteria.name: sse_stream_format
rag_retrieval_grounds_response
criteria.description: When asking a question directly about a seeded video topic (e.g., 'What is retrieval-augmented generation?'), the streamed assistant response references content from the relevant seeded transcript and includes a video title citation matching one of the 10 seeded videos

criteria.name: assistant_message_persisted_after_stream
criteria.description: After the SSE stream from POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages completes, a subsequent GET /api/conversations/{id} returns the full assistant message with role='assistant' and non-empty content stored in the database

criteria.name: updated_at_refreshed_on_message
criteria.description: The updated_at field on a conversation is strictly greater after a new message is sent than it was before; verified by comparing GET /api/conversations response before and after POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages

criteria.name: auto_title_generation
criteria.description: After the first user message is sent to a new conversation, GET /api/conversations returns that conversation with a non-null, non-empty title that is ≤50 characters and is different from the default empty or null title set at creation; sending a second message to the same conversation must NOT overwrite the already-set title

criteria.name: videos_list_endpoint
criteria.description: GET /api/videos returns a JSON array of exactly 10 items (matching seeded data); each item contains id, title, description, url, and created_at fields; HTTP status 200

criteria.name: no_raw_sql_in_routes
criteria.description: All route handlers in conversations.py, messages.py, and ingest.py import and call only repository methods for DB access; no direct aiosqlite cursor or SQL string literals appear in any file under app/backend/routes/

criteria.name: error_body_format
criteria.description: All error responses (404, 422, 500) return a JSON body with at least a 'detail' key containing a human-readable string; no plain-text or HTML error bodies are returned for any defined API route

criteria.name: concurrent_conversations_isolation
criteria.description: Creating two separate conversations and sending distinct messages to each results in GET /api/conversations/{id} returning only the messages belonging to that specific conversation, with no cross-contamination of message history
